revision.text:
'''八坂入媛命'''（やさかいりびめのみこと、生没年不詳）は[[景行天皇]]の[[皇后]]。[[古事記]]には八尺之入日売命（やさかのいりひめのみこと）とある。父は[[八坂入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]]の[[皇子]]）で、母は未詳。[[成務天皇]]・[[五百城入彦皇子]]ら七男六女の母。
景行天皇4年2月21日（74年4月5日）、景行天皇の[[妃]]となった。元々天皇に見初められたのは同母妹の弟媛であったが、固辞した彼女に推薦されて入内に至ったと伝えられる。同52年5月4日（122年6月25日）に当初皇后だった[[播磨稲日大郎姫]]が[[崩御]]したことを受け、同年7月7日（8月26日）に新たな皇后に立てられた。成務天皇2年11月10日（132年12月5日）、[[皇太后]]となった。
== 系譜 == *父：八坂入彦命 *母：未詳 *同母妹：弟媛 *夫：景行天皇 **子：成務天皇・五百城入彦皇子・忍之別皇子・稚倭根子皇子・大酢別皇子・渟熨斗皇女・渟名城皇女・五百城入姫皇女・麛依姫皇女・五十狭城入彦皇子・吉備兄彦皇子・高城入姫皇女・弟姫皇女 **孫：和訶奴気王（成務天皇の皇子）・[[品陀真若王]]（五百城入彦皇子の子。[[応神天皇]]の皇后[[仲姫命]]の父）・吉備郎姫（稚倭根子皇子の子。成務天皇の妃）
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やさかいりひめのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の皇族]]

'''八坂入媛命'''（やさかいりびめのみこと、生没年不詳）は、[[景行天皇]]の[[皇后]]。[[古事記]]には八尺之入日売命（やさかのいりひめのみこと）とある。父は[[八坂入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]]の[[皇子]]）で、母は未詳。[[成務天皇]]・[[五百城入彦皇子]]ら七男六女の母。
景行天皇4年2月21日（74年4月5日）、景行天皇の[[妃]]となった。元々天皇に見初められたのは同母妹の弟媛であったが、固辞した彼女に推薦されて入内に至ったと伝えられる。同52年5月4日（122年6月25日）に当初皇后だった[[播磨稲日大郎姫]]が[[崩御]]したことを受け、同年7月7日（8月26日）に新たな皇后に立てられた。成務天皇2年11月10日（132年12月5日）、[[皇太后]]となった。
== 系譜 == *父：八坂入彦命 *母：未詳 *同母妹：弟媛 *夫：景行天皇 **子：成務天皇・五百城入彦皇子・忍之別皇子・稚倭根子皇子・大酢別皇子・渟熨斗皇女・渟名城皇女・五百城入姫皇女・麛依姫皇女・五十狭城入彦皇子・吉備兄彦皇子・高城入姫皇女・弟姫皇女 **孫：和訶奴気王（成務天皇の皇子）・[[品陀真若王]]（五百城入彦皇子の子。[[応神天皇]]の皇后[[仲姫命]]の父）・吉備郎姫（稚倭根子皇子の子。成務天皇の妃）
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やさかいりひめのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の皇族]] [[Category:生年不明]] [[Category:没年不明]]

'''八坂入媛命'''（やさかいりびめのみこと、生没年不詳）は、[[景行天皇]]の[[皇后]]。[[古事記]]には八尺之入日売命（やさかのいりひめのみこと）とある。父は[[八坂入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]]の[[皇子]]）で、母は未詳。[[成務天皇]]・[[五百城入彦皇子]]ら七男六女の母。
景行天皇4年2月21日（74年4月5日）、景行天皇の[[妃]]となった。元々天皇に見初められたのは同母妹の弟媛であったが、固辞した彼女に推薦されて入内に至ったと伝えられる。同52年5月4日（122年6月25日）に当初皇后だった[[播磨稲日大郎姫]]が[[崩御]]したことを受け、同年7月7日（8月26日）に新たな皇后に立てられた。成務天皇2年11月10日（132年12月5日）、[[皇太后]]となった。

'''八坂入媛命'''（やさかいりびめのみこと、生没年不詳）は、[[景行天皇]]の[[皇后]]。[[古事記]]には八尺之入日売命（やさかのいりひめのみこと）とある。父は[[八坂入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]]の[[皇子]]）で、母は未詳。[[成務天皇]]・[[五百城入彦皇子]]ら七男六女の母。
景行天皇4年2月21日（74年4月5日）、景行天皇の[[妃]]となった。元々天皇に見初められたのは同母妹の弟媛であったが、固辞した彼女に推薦されて入内に至ったと伝えられる。同52年5月4日（122年6月25日）に当初皇后だった[[播磨稲日大郎姫]]が[[崩御]]したことを受け、同年7月7日（8月26日）に新たな皇后に立てられた。成務天皇2年11月10日（132年12月5日）、[[皇太后]]となった。
== 系譜 == *父：八坂入彦命 *母：未詳 *同母妹：弟媛 *夫：景行天皇 **子：成務天皇・五百城入彦皇子・忍之別皇子・稚倭根子皇子・大酢別皇子・渟熨斗皇女・渟名城皇女・五百城入姫皇女・麛依姫皇女・五十狭城入彦皇子・吉備兄彦皇子・高城入姫皇女・弟姫皇女

Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasaka Iribime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, birth and death dates unknown) was the [[Empress]] of [[Emperor Keikō]], the 12th emperor of Japan. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', her name appears as ''Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto''. She was the daughter of [[Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto]] (a son of [[Emperor Sujin]]), and her mother is not recorded. She was the mother of [[Emperor Seimu]] and 13 other children (seven sons and six daughters). She became Emperor Keikō's [[consort]] on the 21st day of the 2nd month in the 4th year of his reign. Originally, Emperor Keikō sought her younger sister, Otome, as his bride, but Otome declined and recommended Yasaka Iribime instead. Following the death of [[Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume]], the first empress, on the 4th day of the 5th month in the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō's reign, Yasaka Iribime was appointed Empress on the 7th day of the 7th month that same year. She was elevated to the status of [[Grand Empress]] on the 10th day of the 11th month in the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == *Father: Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto *Mother: Unknown *Younger Sister: Otome *Husband: Emperor Keikō **Children: - Emperor Seimu - Ioki Iribiko no Ōji - Shinobuno Wakareru no Ōji - Wakaya Maneko no Ōji - Ōsuzuwake no Ōji - Nunoshihime no Ōjo (Nunoshihime no Mikoto) - Nunashiro no Ōjo - Ioki Iribime no Ōjo - Kaiyorihime no Ōjo - Isasakoto Iribiko no Ōji - Kibi no Anehiko no Ōji - Takagi Iribime no Ōjo - Otome no Ōjo {{Imperial Empresses of Japan}} {{Imperial Grand Empresses of Japan}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yasaka Iribime no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese Empresses]] [[Category:Japanese Grand Empresses]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese women]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:dd]]'''Yasakairi-hime''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', her name appears as ''Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto''. She was the daughter of [[Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto]] (a son of [[Emperor Sujin]]), and her mother is not recorded. She was the mother of [[Emperor Seimu]] and 13 other children (seven sons and six daughters). She became Emperor Keikō's [[consort]] on the 21st day of the 2nd month in the 4th year of his reign. Following the death of [[Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume]], the first empress, on the 4th day of the 5th month in the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō's reign, Yasaka Iribime was appointed Empress on the 7th day of the 7th month that same year. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', her name appears as ''Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto''. She was the daughter of [[Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto]] (a son of [[Emperor Sujin]]), and her mother is not recorded. She was the mother of [[Emperor Seimu]] and 13 other children (seven sons and six daughters). She became Emperor Keikō's [[consort]] on the 21st day of the 2nd month in the 4th year of his reign. Originally, Emperor Keikō sought her younger sister, Otome, as his bride, but Otome declined and recommended Yasaka Iribime instead. Following the death of [[Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume]], the first empress, on the 4th day of the 5th month in the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō's reign, Yasaka Iribime was appointed Empress on the 7th day of the 7th month that same year. She was elevated to the status of [[Grand Empress]] on the 10th day of the 11th month in the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == *Father: Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto *Mother: Unknown *Younger Sister: Otome *Husband: Emperor Keikō **Children: - Emperor Seimu - Ioki Iribiko no Ōji - Shinobuno Wakareru no Ōji - Wakaya Maneko no Ōji - Ōsuzuwake no Ōji - Nunoshihime no Ōjo (Nunoshihime no Mikoto) - Nunashiro no Ōjo - Ioki Iribime no Ōjo - Kaiyorihime no Ōjo - Isasakoto Iribiko no Ōji - Kibi no Anehiko no Ōji - Takagi Iribime no Ōjo - Otome no Ōjo {{Imperial Empresses of Japan}} {{Imperial Grand Empresses of Japan}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yasaka Iribime no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese Grand Empresses]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese women]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[en:Yasakairi-hime]][[en:draft:Yasakairi-hime]] [[fa:یاساکاایری هیمه نو میکوتو]] [[zh:八坂入姬命]] [[ko:야사카이리비메노미코토]] [[ja:八坂入媛命]]

Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of Emperor {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇}}. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of Emperor {{ill|Sujin Tennō|ja|崇神天皇}}, while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including Emperor {{ill|Seimu Tennō|ja|成務天皇}}. Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, {{ill|Harima Inahino Ōrōhime|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: Emperor {{ill|Keikō Tennō|ja|景行天皇}} ** **Children**: Emperor {{ill|Seimu Tennō|ja|成務天皇}}, Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]. Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]. Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]. Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Takagi Iribime no Ōjo
Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Yasaka Iribime no Mikoto | consort_reason = Empress of the 12th Emperor | image = | image_width = | alt_text = | caption = | reign_period = | Japanese_reign_period = | Empress_date = 52nd year of [[Emperor Keikō]], 7th month, 7th day | senior_Empress = | Grand Empress = 2nd year of Emperor Seimu, 11th month, 10th day | Great Empress = | Deposed = | Court title = | other_titles = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | funeral = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | naming_date = | former_name = | alternate_titles = | other_names = | childhood_name = | seal = | clan = | father = {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} | mother = Unknown | spouse1 = [[Emperor Keikō]] | spouse2 = | spouse3 = | entered_palace = | marriage = | children = [[Emperor Seimu]]
[[Ioki Iribiko no Ōji]]
Shinobuno Wakareru no Ōji
Wakaya Maneko no Ōji
Ōsuzuwake no Ōji
[[Nunoshihime no Mikoto|Nunoshihime no Ōjo]]
Nunashiro no Ōjo
Ioki Iribime no Ōjo

Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[category:Japanese empresses]] [[category:Japanese empress dowagers]] Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] Shinobuno Wakareru no Ōji
Wakaya Maneko no Ōji
Ōsuzuwake no Ōji
[[Nunoshihime no Mikoto|Nunoshihime no Ōjo]]

Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:Female imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[ja:八坂入媛命]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Consorts of Japan}} Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Yasaka Iribime no Mikoto | consort_reason = Empress of the 12th Emperor | image = | image_width = | alt_text = | caption = | reign_period = | Japanese_reign_period = | Empress_date = 52nd year of [[Emperor Keikō]], 7th month, 7th day | senior_Empress = | Grand Empress = 2nd year of Emperor Seimu, 11th month, 10th day | Great Empress = | Deposed = | Court title = | other_titles = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | funeral = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | naming_date = | former_name = | alternate_titles = | other_names = | childhood_name = | seal = | clan = | father = {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} | mother = Unknown | spouse1 = [[Emperor Keikō]] | spouse2 = | spouse3 = | entered_palace = | marriage = | children = [[Emperor Seimu]]

Shinobuno Wakareru no Ōji

Ōsuzuwake no Ōji
[[Nunoshihime no Mikoto|Nunoshihime no Ōjo]]

Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:Female imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[ja:八坂入媛命]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Consorts of Japan}} Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Yasaka Iribime no Mikoto | consort_reason = Empress of the 12th Emperor | image = | image_width = | alt_text = | caption = | reign_period = | Japanese_reign_period = | Empress_date = 52nd year of [[Emperor Keikō]], 7th month, 7th day | senior_Empress = | Grand Empress = 2nd year of Emperor Seimu, 11th month, 10th day | Great Empress = | Deposed = | Court title = | other_titles = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | funeral = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | naming_date = | former_name = | alternate_titles = | other_names = | childhood_name = | seal = | clan = | father = {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} | mother = Unknown | spouse1 = [[Emperor Keikō]] | spouse2 = | spouse3 = | entered_palace = | marriage = | children = [[Emperor Seimu]]

Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Yasaka Iribime no Mikoto | consort_reason = Empress of the 12th Emperor | image = | image_width = | alt_text = | caption = | reign_period = | Japanese_reign_period = | Empress_date = 52nd year of [[Emperor Keikō]], 7th month, 7th day | senior_Empress = | Grand Empress = 2nd year of Emperor Seimu, 11th month, 10th day | Great Empress = | Deposed = | Court title = | other_titles = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | funeral = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | naming_date = | former_name = | alternate_titles = | other_names = | childhood_name = | seal = | clan = | father = {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} | mother = Unknown | spouse1 = [[Emperor Keikō]] | spouse2 = | spouse3 = | entered_palace = | marriage = | children = [[Emperor Seimu]]

Shinobuno Wakareru no Ōji
Wakaya Maneko no Ōji
Ōsuzuwake no Ōji
[[Nunoshihime no Mikoto|Nunoshihime no Ōjo]]

Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Yasaka Iribime no Mikoto | consort_reason = Empress of the 12th Emperor | image = | image_width = | alt_text = | caption = | reign_period = | Japanese_reign_period = | Empress_date = 52nd year of [[Emperor Keikō]], 7th month, 7th day | senior_Empress = | Grand Empress = 2nd year of Emperor Seimu, 11th month, 10th day | Great Empress = | Deposed = | Court title = | other_titles = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | funeral = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | naming_date = | former_name = | alternate_titles = | other_names = | childhood_name = | seal = | clan = | father = {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki}} | mother = Unknown | spouse1 = [[Emperor Keikō]] | spouse2 = | spouse3 = | entered_palace = | marriage = | children = [[Emperor Seimu]]
[[Ioki Iribiko no Ōji]]
Shinobuno Wakareru no Ōji
Wakaya Maneko no Ōji
Ōsuzuwake no Ōji
[[Nunoshihime no Mikoto|Nunoshihime no Ōjo]]
Nunashiro no Ōjo
Ioki Iribime no Ōjo
Kaiyorihime no Ōjo
Isasakoto Iribiko no Ōji
Kibi no Anehiko no Ōji
Takagi Iribime no Ōjo
Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[simple:Yasakairi-hime]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yasakairi-hime]] {{Consorts of Japan}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yasakairi-hime]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Yasakairi-hime]][[Draft:Yasakairi-hime]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:Female imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese Grand Empresses]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese women]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:cer 5]] [[Category:Yasakairi-hime]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:八坂入媛命]] [[sn:Yasakairi-hime]] [[en:Yasakairi-hime]] [[en:draft:Yasakairi-hime]] [[fa:یاساکاایری هیمه نو میکوتو]] [[zh:八坂入姬命]] [[ko:야사카이리비메노미코토]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yasaka Iribime no Mikoto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yasakairi-hime}}

A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=draft}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|12=draft}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[simple:Yasakairi-hime]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yasakairi-hime]] {{Consorts of Japan}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yasakairi-hime]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Yasakairi-hime]][[Draft:Yasakairi-hime]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:Female imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese Grand Empresses]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese women]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:cer 5]] [[Category:Yasakairi-hime]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:八坂入媛命]] [[sn:Yasakairi-hime]] [[en:Yasakairi-hime]] [[en:draft:Yasakairi-hime]] [[fa:یاساکاایری هیمه نو میکوتو]] [[zh:八坂入姬命]] [[ko:야사카이리비메노미코토]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yasaka Iribime no Mikoto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yasakairi-hime}}

Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=draft|12=draft}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=draft|12=draft}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} ==Genealogy== {{Generations of {{出典の明記|date=2017-08-03}} {{基礎情報 皇后 |名 = 八坂入媛命 |立后根拠 = 第12代天皇后 |画像 = |画像幅 = |画像代替文 = |画像説明 = |在位期間 = |和暦在位期間 = |皇后 = 景行天皇52年7月7日 |上皇后 = |皇太后 = 成務天皇2年11月10日 |太皇太后 = |廃后 = |女院号 = |女院 = |出生日 = 不明 |生地 = |死亡日 = 不明 |没地 = |大喪儀 = |陵所 = |諱 = |命名年月日 = |旧名 = |諡号 = |異称 = |戒名 = |幼称 = |印 = |氏族 = |父親 = [[八坂入彦命]] |母親 = |配偶者1 = [[景行天皇]] |配偶者2 = |配偶者3 = |入内 = |結婚 = |子女 = [[成務天皇]]
[[五百城入彦皇子]]
忍之別皇子
稚倭根子皇子
大酢別皇子
[[渟熨斗姫命|渟熨斗皇女]]
渟名城皇女
五百城入姫皇女
麛依姫皇女
五十狭城入彦皇子
吉備兄彦皇子
高城入姫皇女
弟姫皇女 |養子女 = |女御宣下 = |皇太夫人 = |准后 = |身位 = |皇居 = |宮廷首脳人物 = |宮廷首脳人物2 = |宮廷女房 = |地位 = |立后前位階 = |栄典 = |役職 = |親署 = |脚注 = }} '''八坂入媛命'''（やさかいりびめのみこと、生没年不詳）は、[[景行天皇]]の[[皇后]]。[[古事記]]には八尺之入日売命（やさかのいりひめのみこと）とある。父は[[八坂入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]]の[[皇子]]）で、母は未詳。[[成務天皇]]・[[五百城入彦皇子]]ら七男六女の母。 景行天皇4年2月21日、景行天皇の[[妃]]となった。元々天皇に見初められたのは同母妹の弟媛であったが、固辞した彼女に推薦されて入内に至ったと伝えられる。同52年5月4日に当初皇后だった[[播磨稲日大郎姫]]が[[崩御]]したことを受け、同年7月7日に新たな皇后に立てられた。成務天皇2年11月10日、[[皇太后]]となった。 == 系譜 == *父：八坂入彦命 *母：未詳 *同母妹：弟媛 *夫：景行天皇 **子：成務天皇・五百城入彦皇子・忍之別皇子・稚倭根子皇子・大酢別皇子・渟熨斗皇女([[渟熨斗姫命]])・渟名城皇女・五百城入姫皇女・麛依姫皇女・五十狭城入彦皇子・吉備兄彦皇子・高城入姫皇女・弟姫皇女 {{歴代皇后一覧}} {{歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やさかいりひめのみこと}} [[Category:日本の皇后]] [[Category:日本の皇太后]] [[Category:景行天皇]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]
