Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{Consorts of Japan}} [[Draft:Yasakairi-hime]] {{translated page|ja|八坂入媛命|version=87683736|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yasakairi-hime]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese empress dowagers]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:Female imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese Grand Empresses]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese women]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yasakairi-hime]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[simple:Yasakairi-hime]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yasakairi-hime]] [[sn:Yasakairi-hime]] [[ja:八坂入媛命]] [[en:Yasakairi-hime]] [[en:draft:Yasakairi-hime]] [[fa:یاساکاایری هیمه نو میکوتو]] [[zh:八坂入姬命]] [[ko:야사카이리비메노미코토]]

She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. {{Infobox royalty | name = Yasaka Iribime no Mikoto | consort_reason = Empress of the 12th Emperor | image = | image_width = | alt_text = | caption = | reign_period = | Japanese_reign_period = | Empress_date = 52nd year of [[Emperor Keikō]], 7th month, 7th day | senior_Empress = | Grand Empress = 2nd year of Emperor Seimu, 11th month, 10th day | Great Empress = | Deposed = | Court title = | other_titles = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | funeral = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | naming_date = | former_name = | alternate_titles = | other_names = | childhood_name = | seal = | clan = | father = {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | mother = Unknown | spouse1 = [[Emperor Keikō]] | spouse2 = | spouse3 = | entered_palace = | marriage = | children = [[Emperor Seimu]]

Shinobuno Wakareru no Ōji
Wakaya Maneko no Ōji
Ōsuzuwake no Ōji
[[Nunoshihime no Mikoto|Nunoshihime no Ōjo]]
Nunashiro no Ōjo
Ioki Iribime no Ōjo
Kaiyorihime no Ōjo
Isasakoto Iribiko no Ōji
Kibi no Anehiko no Ōji
Takagi Iribime no Ōjo
Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|qq=}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Consorts of Japan}} revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Yasaka Iribime no Mikoto | consort_reason = Empress of the 12th Emperor | image = | image_width = | alt_text = | caption = | reign_period = | Japanese_reign_period = | Empress_date = 52nd year of [[Emperor Keikō]], 7th month, 7th day | senior_Empress = | Grand Empress = 2nd year of Emperor Seimu, 11th month, 10th day | Great Empress = | Deposed = | Court title = | other_titles = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | funeral = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | naming_date = | former_name = | alternate_titles = | other_names = | childhood_name = | seal = | clan = | father = {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | mother = Unknown | spouse1 = [[Emperor Keikō]] | spouse2 = | spouse3 = | entered_palace = | marriage = | children = [[Emperor Seimu]]

Wakaya Maneko no Ōji
Ōsuzuwake no Ōji
[[Nunoshihime no Mikoto|Nunoshihime no Ōjo]]
Nunashiro no Ōjo
Ioki Iribime no Ōjo

Kibi no Anehiko no Ōji
Takagi Iribime no Ōjo
Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|qq=}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Consorts of Japan}} Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{Infobox royalty | name = Yasaka Iribime no Mikoto | consort_reason = Empress of the 12th Emperor | image = | image_width = | alt_text = | caption = | reign_period = | Japanese_reign_period = | Empress_date = 52nd year of [[Emperor Keikō]], 7th month, 7th day | senior_Empress = | Grand Empress = 2nd year of Emperor Seimu, 11th month, 10th day | Great Empress = | Deposed = | Court title = | other_titles = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | funeral = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | naming_date = | former_name = | alternate_titles = | other_names = | childhood_name = | seal = | clan = | father = {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | mother = Unknown | spouse1 = [[Emperor Keikō]] | spouse2 = | spouse3 = | entered_palace = | marriage = | children = [[Emperor Seimu]]

Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of [[Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{Consorts of Japan}} [[Draft:Yasakairi-hime]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|八坂入媛命|version=87683736|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Yasakairi-hime]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese women]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Link complete]] }} [[simple:Yasakairi-hime]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yasakairi-hime]] [[sn:Yasakairi-hime]] [[ja:八坂入媛命]] [[fa:یاساکاایری هیمه نو میکوتو]] [[zh:八坂入姬命]] [[ko:야사카이리비메노미코토]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]] [[Category:日本の皇太后]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{Consorts of Japan}} [[Draft:Yasakairi-hime]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|八坂入媛命|version=87683736|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Yasakairi-hime]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:Year of birth and death unknown]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese women]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] }} [[simple:Yasakairi-hime]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yasakairi-hime]] [[sn:Yasakairi-hime]] [[ja:八坂入媛命]] [[fa:یاساکاایری هیمه نو میکوتو]] [[zh:八坂入姬命]] [[ko:야사카이리비메노미코토]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]] [[Category:日本の皇太后]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this link invalid}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[:en:Toyo Bunko|Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[:en:Toyo Bunko|Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[:en:Toyo Bunko|Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[:en:Toyo Bunko|Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[:en:Toyo Bunko|Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[:en:Toyo Bunko|Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[:en:Toyo Bunko|Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|List of Japanese Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[:en:Toyo Bunko|Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Consorts of Japan}} Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[:en:Toyo Bunko|Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Consorts of Japan}} Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[:en:Toyo Bunko|Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Consorts of Japan}} Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[:en:Toyo Bunko|Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Consorts of Japan}} Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. She is also known in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[:en:Toyo Bunko|Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Consorts of Japan}} She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] ** **Children**: [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Consorts of Japan}} Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}, while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[:en:Toyo Bunko|Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}} ** **Children**: {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}} Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja_comment=jawiki link Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}, while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[:en:Toyo Bunko|Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}} ** **Children**: {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}} Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[:en:Toyo Bunko|Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} ** **Children**: {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}} Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese Empresses|ja|歴代皇后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empresses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Empress Dowagers|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} ==Genealogy== Otome no Ōjo | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Yasakairibime no Mikoto''' (八坂入媛命, やさかいりびめのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was the consort of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}. She is also known in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} as "Yasaka no Iribime no Mikoto" (八尺之入日売命). Her father was {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, while her mother is unknown. She was the mother of seven sons and six daughters, including {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}..{{Cite web|title=Yasakairihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/yasakairihime/|access-date=2023-11-28|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{cite book|author=Kenneth Henshall|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Seimu+84&pg=PA487|title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945|publisher=Scarecrow Press|year=2013|isbn=9780810878723|page=487}}{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Wakatarashi+hiko|title=Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34|publisher=[[:en:Toyo Bunko|Toyo Bunko]]|year=1974|pages=63–64|access-date=1 August 2019}} Yasakairibime entered the court of Emperor Keikō on February 21 of the 4th year of his reign. Originally, her younger sister Otome had caught the Emperor's attention, but Otome declined and recommended Yasakairibime, leading to her union with the Emperor. On May 4 of the 52nd year of Keikō's reign, the then-empress, [[Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]], passed away. Yasakairibime was elevated to empress on July 7 of the same year. She became Empress Dowager on November 10 of the 2nd year of Emperor Seimu's reign. == Genealogy == * **Father**: {{ill|Yasakairibiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this **Mother**: Unknown * **Younger Sister**: Otome * **Husband**: {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} ** **Children**: {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}} Iokiribiko no Mikoto, Osobewake no Mikoto, Wakayaonekoneko no Mikoto, Ōsubewake no Mikoto, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Nunashirohime no Mikoto, Iokiriihime no Mikoto, Kainoyorihime no Mikoto, Isasajiiwakohiko no Mikoto, Kibinoanihiko no Mikoto, Takagiihime no Mikoto, and Otohime no Mikoto. {{ill|List of Japanese
